assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md lines 9-16: Grouping strategy 'by-repository' with Epic Hierarchy table listing Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend) and Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui), both with Parent TC-9006 and Issue Type Epic. Two Epics created, one per repository.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic Hierarchy table: Epic 1 summary is 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', Epic 2 summary is 'TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Both follow the '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' format exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Task-to-Epic Assignment table (lines 69-81): Tasks 1-3 (trustify-backend tasks) are assigned to 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' Epic; Tasks 4-8 (trustify-ui tasks) are assigned to 'TC-9006: trustify-ui' Epic. Confirmed in summary-comment.md lines 7-14 with matching Epic assignments.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Incorporates links from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks) — evidenced by link decisions in the impact map or task output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 17: 'Incorporates links: Feature TC-9006 incorporates Epic 1 and Epic 2 (not individual Tasks).' summary-comment.md line 23: 'Incorporates links: Feature TC-9006 incorporates each Epic (not individual Tasks).'

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are Tasks 1-3 (trustify-backend), frontend tasks are Tasks 4-8 (trustify-ui). Task 4 (first frontend task) lists dependencies on Tasks 1 and 2. Task 5 depends on Task 4. Task 6 depends on Task 5. Task 7 depends on Tasks 5 and 6. Task 8 depends on Tasks 1-7. All backend task numbers (1-3) are lower than all frontend task numbers (4-8), preserving dependency ordering.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-doc/non-test tasks (1, 2, 4, 5, 6) all contain: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Testing tasks (3: 'integration tests', 7: 'tests for remediation dashboard') contain all required sections including the exempt ones. Documentation task (8: 'Document remediation dashboard') contains Repository (trustify-ui), Target Branch (main), Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — exempt sections (Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes) are correctly omitted.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Epic creation with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key — evidenced by Epic creation parameters in the impact map or output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 10: 'Level-1 issue type: Epic'. Epic Hierarchy table shows Issue Type 'Epic' and Parent 'TC-9006' for both Epics. Lines 62-63: 'Issue type: Epic (hierarchyLevel 1)' and 'Parent: TC-9006'. summary-comment.md Epics Created table: both epics show 'Epic (hierarchyLevel 1)' and Parent 'TC-9006'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 task files have a '## Repository' section with a single repository name: Tasks 1-3 have 'trustify-backend', Tasks 4-8 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 task files contain '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria', and '## Test Requirements' sections. Verified in task-1 through task-8 markdown files.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks have separate digest files with valid sha256-md: prefixed 64-char hex hashes: task-1-digest: sha256-md:c49efd98b11187f95bf8a8528e8f676f8b9bdf3a119774a88a01963a03660e5d, task-2-digest: sha256-md:fd2e738c4cd1ff5bb00d0b2917c77a563c368af3477e629905ef675834e9fbdf, task-3-digest: sha256-md:3774c3a967770aa50af5f4f89e8c0f43a778321e23fe9029eb0f795b19ed217a, task-4-digest: sha256-md:7fc9799a0d47b0cab89190cf52225b56efeb1af47e7b41ece1b19dc388aae38d, task-5-digest: sha256-md:2494f0f6c688863f635b40cd3cd0318373affa9ee00a9902a01124c5fe856917, task-6-digest: sha256-md:623db27f9a3d238e957fd140855395e0f25b5d930cc4a9b9f065241205f9c980, task-7-digest: sha256-md:4e850e03fd15399887eb8a51ed5bc0c2f9b84d92b7b5ed49ea82bdaecba00102, task-8-digest: sha256-md:7a80559dd4cf6b73082095874892ac99cf8e780e02d2b2c2b5ef440ac378c30d. Each contains the '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' marker.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All task files with conventions use the prescribed format. Task 1 Implementation Notes: 'Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/remediation/endpoints/mod.rs matching the convention's module structure scope', 'Applies: task creates modules/fundamental/src/remediation/endpoints/summary.rs matching the convention's Rust endpoint file scope', 'Applies: task modifies server/src/main.rs matching the convention's endpoint registration scope', etc. Tasks 2-7 follow the same pattern. No conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. Task 8 (documentation) contains no convention annotations, consistent with no applicable conventions for documentation scope.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 4: 'Priority: Major'. Line 67: 'Priority: {"name": "Major"} (inherited from Feature)'. summary-comment.md lines 41-42: 'Feature priority: Major' and 'Propagated to tasks: Yes — all tasks and Epics created with priority: {"name": "Major"}'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 5: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0'. Line 68: 'Fix Versions: [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}] (inherited from Feature; fixVersion scope defaults to "both")'. summary-comment.md lines 44-47: 'Feature fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0', 'Propagated to tasks: Yes — all tasks and Epics created with fixVersions: [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]', 'fixVersion scope: Not configured in Jira Field Defaults (defaults to "both" — propagate to tasks)'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in outputs directory with '## Inherited Field Values' section (lines 39-57) containing: '### Priority' subsection documenting 'Feature priority: Major' propagated to all tasks/Epics; '### Fix Versions' subsection documenting 'Feature fixVersions: RHTPA 1.5.0' propagated to all tasks/Epics; and '### additional_fields applied to each created issue' JSON block showing labels, priority, and fixVersions values.
